EX89 – Ecom Document printing and Gel Pack Logic During certain times of year, for ecommerce orders, McLane has a need to include gel packs in the containers that are being shipped. This is to keep the contents from thawing/melting. McLane currently has a gel pack logic called the BOLDTSMITH logic which is being used to determine how many gel packs are needed in a container based off the containers contents as well as the weather forecast. In order for WM to leverage this logic t his extension allows WM to integrate with McLane’s Ecommerce system via REST calls to their APIs to request the number of gel packs for a container when needed. For McLane’s ecommerce orders , base ecom EPI as well as base parcel carrier logic is not used. Instead, WM is enhanced to integrate with McLane’s Ecommerce system via REST calls to deliver the appropriate information to McLane’s ecommerce system which will then connect to the appropriate parcel carriers and send WM back the shipping information via the appropriate outbound documentation . WM is also enhanced to take this information and submit it for printing. Specifically, WM’s Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced to make the REST calls to McLane’s Ecommerce system . The Weigh and Manifest UI is also enhanced to display the recommended number of gel packs as well as check against custom weight thresholds. The UI is further enhanced to allow users to enter in the number of gel packs , change the containe r type , and also update the weight if deemed necessary . Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Weigh and Manifest UI MHE N RF Y RF Print OB Docs Reporting N Other Y LPN table , 5 custom fields are created For both the Ecom Multi Flow and the Ecom Singles Flow the Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced to make the REST call to the Ecom/Gel Pack API at the time of oLPN scan to receive the calculated number of gel packs that the BOLTSMITH logic has determined. This call is only made if the LPN needs gel packs. The custom weight thresholds for the carriers are also validated in this logic. I f any Errors are sent back to WM , then the errors will be shown to the user and the user will need to try to scan the LPN again after having taken the appropriate actions . The Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced to include new fields for the different gel pack sizes in order to display to the user what gel packs are needed for the container. The user has the ability to edit these fields and the container weight will update appropriately upon edit. The Weigh and Manifest Weigh button is leveraged to make the REST call to send McLane’s ecommerce system the appropriate information to receive back the outbound documentation. Clicking the Weigh button also triggers some custom weight validations. If the base and custom validations pass , then WM makes the REST call to receive back the outbound documentation. D epending on the response , WM either shows the appropriate error for the failed response or indicates to the user that the job has been submitted for printing . If the response is received back with any errors or there are any errors in the communication, then an error message is displayed to the user and the user will need to use the RF Print OB Docs transaction later on once the error has been resolved. The RF Print OB Docs transaction is also enhanced to make the REST call to McLane in order to attain the appropriate information for printing of the OB documents. The users will be able to choose which documents they want to print with the RF Print OB Docs transaction . Assumptions All Ecom orders have order type as “ ECOM ” For Ecom orders McLane Hub connect s to Ecom API which runs the Boldtsmith logic and send s the order line level gel pack volume needed for that order line Only Ecom orders will be bridged into WM in “Draft” status . Ecom orders are bridged in with a valid Ship Via EX88 is enabled and is used to move the orders into “Released” status. This modification only applies to LPNs which are part of the Ecom flow . Communication between WM and McLane’s Ecommerce system is done through REST APIs where the communication will take place in JSON file format. McLane’s Ecommerce system has appropriately set up the correct REST APIs for WM to connect to. The PDF documents sent in the JSON file are in Base 64 encoding . The ZPL file sent in the JSON file is sent in a ZPL string . For the Outbound Documentation API REST call t he response JSON contains a separate Key value pair for the concatenated PDF, the shipping label, the Hazmat PDF (if needed ), the invoice, and the ship via. WM printer /print requestor configuration is setup properly to print the Ecom Outbound Documents System code C-89 G is correctly configured, with the code id descriptions matching the gel pack sizes which the ecommerce system will send back in their JSON file response. And with the appropriate code ids enabled. Gel pack weight is specified in system code C-8 9 G param 2 - 5 Currently there is a limit of 5 configurable gel pack sizes for a given division Weight limits for containers are configured in system code C-E89 f or each defined ship via , where the code id represents a ship via name . Shipping labels will always be printed on adhesive label s via a label printer. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX88 population of ORDER_LINE_ITEM. INTERNAL_ORDER_SEQ_ NBR Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram Not required for this extension UI Weigh and Manifest UI The Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced to call the Ecom/Gel Pack API whenever a n LPN which needs gel packs is scanned. The number of gel packs will be displayed to the user in new custom fields on the right-hand side of the UI Weigh page . These fields will be editable, and if a user would like to change the number of gel packs they can do so via these fields. The fields represent the different gel pack sizes which are set up in system code C-89G. WM is enhanced to show users a message whenever they make a change to the gel pack fields . When they make a change, WM update s the number of gel packs in the container as well as the containers weight in accordance with the increase or decrease in the number of gel packs. Additionally , the Weigh and Manifest UI W eigh button is enhanced to first carry out base validations and updates and then carry out custom weigh validations. Once these updates occur and validations pass , WM make s a second REST call to a different Ecom/Gel Pack API in order to send the McLane’s Ecommerce system the appropriate information needed in order for the Ecommerce system to communicate with the appropriate logistics party and generate the o utbound documentation . The outbound documentation is sent to WM in JSON file and depending on the information WM prints the appropriate documents. If the LPN is the last LPN for a shipment, then WM indicates this to McLane’s Ecommerce system and WM will receive back the Invoice for the entire order along with the other document s for th at LPN. If there are any errors during the REST call or included in the REST call’s response, WM displays this to the user. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX89 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX89 is turned off, and no custom EX89 processing will occur. Lastly, upon LPN scan WM checks to see if the LPN belongs to an Order w ith an Order Type of ECOM. If the LPN is associated with an ECOM order , then the below changes are carried out. If not , then no custom EX89 processing occurs. UI Format Changes The Weigh and Manifest UI has three distinct screens : oLPN prompt screen Weigh screen LPN summary screen Weigh Screen Changes If the above enablement is true , then the Weigh screen is enhanced in the following ways: The Ship Via field is enhanced to be un-editable. WM checks system code C-89G code IDs parameter 1-1 . For the Code IDs that have this enablement parameter checked WM displays the Code ID description as well as a n input numeric box next to the description. If the Enablement parameter is not checked then the description and fields will NOT be displayed in the UI. A total of 5 descriptions and input boxes can be displayed. Code ID 001 will be associated with the first input box, code ID 002 to the 2 nd input box, and so on and so forth. The field description comes from the corresponding code id description. The descriptions define the gel pack size and need to match the sizes sent back to WM in the Gel Pack API call. The input boxes are populated with the information sent back to WM in the Gel Pack API call. Section 4.1.3.3 details out this process. Users also can update these values, this is explained in section 4.1.4.1 LPN Summary Screen changes If the above enablement is true , the LPN Summary screen is enhanced in two ways. The first is an indica tor to users that a Tobacco/Cigarette sticker is needed on the container (as shown below at bottom of UI ). For the indicator to be displayed to the user , WM first checks to see if there are any Tobacco or Cigarette items in the container. This is done by seeing if any item has a prod_type that matches the prod type specific in C-89T Code IDs. If the container does not contain an item with matching prod_ types, then the indicator is not displayed. If the container does contain an item with a matching prod_type, WM then checks to see if the container is associated with an Order which has the destination state matching a code ID configured in system code C-89 S. If the state does not match a code ID , then WM does not display the indicator. If the state does match , then WM displays the indicator. The second enhancement to the screen is to display the gel pack quantity summary information. As shown in the below screen design , the summary of the gel pack quantities can be seen on the right side of the screen and show the quantities that the user has confirmed with the Weigh button. The gel pack summary is based off the configuration of the input fields and the values. LPN Scan The LPN scan on the Weight and Manifest UI is enhanced in such a way that if a n oLPN is needing gel packs, once the user scans the LPN, WM makes a REST call to McLane’s Ecommerce system in order to receive back the number of gel packs recommended by the BOLDTSMITH logic. Validation When a n LPN is scanned WM carries out the base LPN validations. If the base LPN validations pass then WM checks to see if LPN is part of an ECOM order, Then WM check s to see if any of the details on the LPN are associated with an order line that has ORDER_LINE_ITEM. INTERNAL_ORDER_SEQ_ NBR > 0. If so , WM makes the REST call to the Gel Pack API. If not , then WM continues with base process and no REST call is made. Synchronous REST call Once the LPN is scanned and the above validations pass, WM gathers and sends out the below LPN information in JSON file format through a REST call to the Gel Pack API. Ecom order number ( ORDER _LINE_ITEM .EXT_PURCHASE_ORDER ) associated with the LPN LPN ID LPN Details (items and quantities) LPN Container size Technical Note: To make the REST call the following authorization and authentication is required. Key Cloak O-AUTH Token based authentication If a successful connection and authorization is made , WM receives back the response of the REST call in a JSON file format . Based on the response the below updates are carried out. If the response does not contain an error , then t he response will contain the number of gel packs for each gel pack size for that container . WM takes th ese number s and carries out the updates outlined in the update section below. If the response does contain an e rror , then WM displays CUST89 15 Message to the user. The CUST8904 M e ssage will display the Error ID and Message found in the JSON file as well as letting the users know to “ for LPN {X}. Please use RF Print OB Docs to retry print and/or Contact Help Desk ” WM records the error message in the MSG_LOG table If the connection to the API was unsuccessful then the following error messages are shown to the user. If due to HTTP Error 400 display CUST89 12 If due to HTTP Error 500 display CUST89 13 If due to HTTP Error 404 display CUST89 14 WM also records these error message in the MSG_LOG table Updates WM first updates the custom table API_TRANSACTION_LOG with the appropriate information from the REST call. With a successful REST call where no errors are returned , WM receives back the number of gel packs for each gel pack size for that container. W M first does the Weight updates below. If the weight updates do not result in a split message, then WM continues with the Gel Pack updates . Weight updates WM attempts to update the LPN’s estimated weight. WM does this by taking the current LPN estimated weight and then adding the weight of the gel packs . When WM receives the JSON file from the successful REST call, the file will contain the different gel pack sizes and their associated quantities. WM determines the weight of the gel packs by matching the gel pack size sent in the JSON file with the C-89G code id description for that size and taking the quantity for that size that is provided in the JSON file and multiplying by the configured weight for that gel pack size. The weight defined in code id’s param eter 2- 5 . This is done for all the sizes withing the JSON file. If there is a size in the JSON file which is not found within the system code C-89G code ID description, then WM does not carry out the update to the estimated weight and the CUST 891 6 message is shown to the user stating that the system code is not configured for the gel pack size. If all the sizes in the JSON file do match and WM is able to calculate the weight of all the gel pack sizes, it takes th e sum of all these weights and add s this to the LPN’s current estimated weight to get the updated estimated weight . If the calculated weight exceeds the base max weight tolerance set for the container type AND/OR the weight limit set for the container’s associated Ship Via , then WM does not carry out the update to the estimated weight and displays to the user the CUST8908 Error Message which asks the user to split the container since it has exceeded the weight limit. The weight limits associated to the ship via’s are con figured in system code C-E89, where the Code ID is the ship via name and the weight limit is from parameters 1-4 of that code id. If the calculated weight does NOT exceed the limit set for the container’s associated Ship Via, then WM updates the LPN’s estimated weight (LPN. ESTIMATED_WEIGHT ) as well as the LPN’s actual weight (LPN.WEIGHT) with the newly calculated weight . WM then continues with the Gel Pack updates below . After the Gel Pack updates below are carried out and the user is brought to the Weigh screen of the Weigh and Manifest UI the user is able to see the LPN’s actual and estimated weight in the appropriate fields. Gel Pack U pdates The LPN table is customized to add 5 new fields called GEL_PACK_001, GEL_PACK_002, GEL_PACK_003, GEL_PACK_004, GEL_PACK_005 . These fields are associated with the Code IDs from system code C-89G. When W M receives the JSON file from the successful REST call, the file will contain the different gel pack sizes and their associated quantities. The C-89G code id description is to match the gel pack size sent in the JSON file . The check for the match is done in the Weight update section above. WM looks at the JSON file and updates the appropriate LPN .GEL_PACK_XXX field with the quantity provided in the JSON file , matching the size of the gel pack in the JSON file to the code ID description and the code id number to the appropriate LPN.GEL_PACK_XXX number. The gel pack input fields defined in section 4.1.2 are associated with the appropriate LPN.GEL_PACK_XXX fields and when WM displays the Weigh screen the input fields will be populated accordingly. Navigation If base validations fail, then user is shown the base error message and once the message is accepted the user is brought back to the Weigh and Manifest Weigh screen . If the custom weight updates result in a split message, then when the user accepts the message, the user is brought back to the Weigh and Manifest oLPN prompt screen. If all validations pass and the weight updates are below the threshold and the REST call is unsuccessful an Error message is shown, when the user accepts the message, they are brought back to the Weigh and Manifest oLPN prompt screen. If all validations pass and the weight updates are below the threshold and the REST call is successful and errors are received in the response, then the user is shown the error message. when the user accepts the error message they are brought back to the Weigh and Manifest oLPN prompt screen. If all validations pass and the weight updates are below the threshold and the REST call is successful with no errors, then the user is brought to the Weigh and Manifest Weigh page , which will give the user all the appropriate information. Weight Screen Actions In the Weigh screen the users have the ability to change the number of gel packs for each gel pack size. The users also have the ability to edit the Actual weight. Changing Gel Pack Quantity If a user decides to change the number of gel packs in the container, they can do so updating the number of gel packs for each available gel pack size . If the user decreases the number of gel packs on any of the sizes (quantity entered < LPN. GEL_PACK_XXX ) then WM displays a the CUST8909 message. When the user clicks OK on the message then WM updates the appropriate LPN.GEL_PACK_XXX field with the user entered quantity. Additionally, WM recalculates the actual weight of the LPN. WM updates the LPN’s actual weight by taking the current actual weight of the LPN (LPN.WEIGHT) and subtracting the weight of the removed gel packs. Technical note: to calculate the removed gel pack weight , take the difference in gel packs and multiply by the appropriate gel pack size’s gel pack weight specified in the system code C-E8 9-XXX parameter 2 - 5 . If the user increases the number of gel packs on any of the sizes (quantity entered > LPN.GEL_PACK_XXX) then WM displays a the CUST89 10 message. When the user clicks OK on the message then WM updates the appropriate LPN.GEL_PACK_XXX field with the user entered quantity. Additionally, WM recalculates the actual weight of the LPN. WM updates the LPN’s actual weight by taking the current actual weight of the LPN (LPN.WEIGHT) and adding the weight of the additional gel packs. Technical note: to calculate the additional gel pack weight, take the difference in gel packs and multiply by the appropriate gel pack size’s gel pack weight specified in the system code C-E8 9-XXX parameter 2 - 5 . If the user changes the gel pack quantity back to the original suggested gel pack quantity, then no message is to be displayed to the user. Technical note: We will need to temporarily store the original gel pack quantity somewhere for the UI session so that i f they do change the quantity and the above logic is invoked then we will loose the original gel pack quantity from the LPN.GEL_PACK_XXX field. NOTE: The weight validations will only occur once the user clicks on the Weigh button. See Weigh Button section below for validations. Updating the Actual Weight If a user decides to update the weight of the container , then they can do so by editing the actual weight field in the Weigh and Manifest Weigh page. NOTE: The field can be updated but the weight validations will only occur once the user clicks on the Weigh button. See Weigh Button section below for validations. Weight Button The Weigh button on the Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced in such a way that, once the user scans the LPN, WM makes a REST call to McLane’s Ecommerce system in order to send the Ecommerce system the information about the packed LPN including the number of gel packs packed (if available) as well as requesting the outbound documentation from the Ecommerce system. Validation WM carries out its base validations for the Weigh button. If the base validations pass, then WM checks to see if the LPN’s actual weight (LPN.WEIGHT) exceeds the weight limit set for the container’s associated Ship Via . If the calculated weight exceeds the weight limit set for the container’s associated Ship Via, then WM does not carry out the update to the estimated weight and displays to the user the CUST8908 Error Message which asks the user to split the container since it has exceeded the weight limit. The weight limits associated to the ship via’s are configured in system code C-E89, where the Code ID is the ship via name and the weight limit is from parameters 1-4 of that code id. If the calculated weight does not exceed the weight limit , then WM continues with the base updates for the LPN . Once the base updates are carried out then WM continues with the below REST call. Synchronous REST call Once the Weigh button is clicked, WM gathers and sends out the below LPN information in a JSON file through a REST call to the Get Documents API. Ecom order number (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.EXT_PURCHASE_ORDER) associated with the LPN LPN ID # of gel packs by size (if applicable, WM sends 0 for each size if no gel packs needed) LPN container size LPN container weight Items and quantity Last LPN of the shipment indicator (Y or N) WM checks to see if the LPN that has been packed is the last LPN that is less than Weighed status. If the LPN is the last LPN, then WM populates this Last LPN indicator as Y If it is not the last LPN, then WM populates the Last LPN indicator as N Document type indicator = 01 The values that can be sent on this field are defined in system code C- 89 R Code ID 01 is the Print ALL documents configuration. Technical Note: In order to make the REST call the following authorization and authentication is required. Key Cloak O-AUTH Token based authentication With a successful connection and authorization made, WM receives back the response of the REST call in a JSON file format. The contents of the JSON file and how WM handles them is defined in the 4. 1.5.4 Printing section below. If the response does contain an error, then WM displays CUST8904 Message to the user. The CUST8904 Message will display the Error ID and Message found in the JSON file as well as letting the users know to “ for LPN {X}. Please use RF Print OB Docs to retry print and/or Contact Help Desk ” WM records the error message in the MSG_LOG table If the connection to the API was unsuccessful then the following error messages are shown to the user. If due to HTTP Error 400 display CUST8901 If due to HTTP Error 500 display CUST8902 If due to HTTP Error 404 display CUST8903 WM also records these error message in the MSG_LOG table Updates WM first updates the custom table API_TRANSACTION_LOG with the appropriate information from the REST call. After the Weight button is selected and validations pass, then before the REST call is made, base updates are carried out for the LPN . A separate Key value pair in the JSON file will be sent with the final Ship Via the ecommerce system has determined to use. WM will take this value and update the LPN’s Ship Via accordingly before printing the Outbound documents. Printing The information within the JSON file that McLane’s ecommerce system sends WM is based on two parameters that are sent on the REST call to the ecom system. The first is the document type indicator. For the REST call associated with the weigh button the document type indicator is always set to 001 which calls for all documentation. This includes the following: A key value pair for the Packing list PDF file. The PDF file is in base 64 encoding. This file includes but is not limited to Packing List Greeting Tobacco information If the container contains Hazmat items, then McLane’s ecommerce system includes the Hazmat key value pair along with the Hazmat PDF file in base 64 encoding. A ZPL file which is sent as a ZPL string. This is the shipping label associated with the container. The second parameter is the Last LPN indicator. If WM sends the L ast LPN indicator = Y, then McLane’s ecommerce system includes another key value pair for the Invoice of the shipment . The Invoice is in PDF file which is in base 64 encoding . If the response JSON file does not include the Hazmat key value pair , then once the files are sent to th eir respective print requestors, WM loads the Weigh and Manifest LPN summary page which prompts for the next LPN and displays ( on top of the page) the CUST8905 Info message indicating that the job was submitted for printing. If the response JSON file DOES include the Hazmat key value pair, then once the files are sent to their respective print requestors, WM loads the Weigh and Manifest LPN summary page which prompts for the next LPN and displays ( on top of the page) the CUST89 07 Info message indicating that there is Hazmat documentation to be printed and the Print Hazmat button needs to be used. Navigation Once a user selects the Weigh button If base validations fail , then user is shown the base error message . Once the user exits out of the message they remain on the Weigh and Manifest Weigh screen . If the custom weight validation fail s, then the user is shown the custom CUST8908 Error message. Once the user exits out of the message they remain on the Weigh and Manifest Weigh screen. If all validations pass and the REST call is unsuccessful an Error message is shown . Once the user exits out of the message they remain on the Weigh and Manifest Weigh screen. If all validations pass , the REST call is successful , and errors are received in the response, then the user is shown the error message. Once the user exits out of the message they are brought to the Weigh and Manifest oLPN Summary screen . If all validations pass , the REST call is successful , no errors are received in the response, and there is NO hazmat key value pa ir in the JSON file, then the user is brought to the Weigh and Manifest oLPN Summary page and shown the CUST8905 job sent to printer info message. Once the user exits out of the message they are brought to the Weigh and Manifest oLPN Summary screen and p rompted for the next oLPN. This is a prompt at the top of the page. If all validations pass, the REST call is successful, no errors are received in the response, and there is a hazmat key value pair in the JSON file, then the user is brought to the Weigh and Manifest oLPN Summary page and shown the CUST89 07 m essage that the container contains hazmat and th at the y will need to use the RF Print OB docs transaction to print the hazmat documentation. Once the user exits out of the message they are brought to the Weigh and Manifest oLPN Summary screen and p rompted for the next oLPN. This is a prompt at the top of the page. Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking is needed for the Weigh and Manifest UI as part of this extension. PIX No PIX needed for this transaction. LM Hooks No changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. RF RF Print OB Docs The RF Print OB Docs transaction is leveraged to print hazmat documentation if needed as well as print /re-print documentation for an LPN. W hether in the Ecom Multi Flow or the Ecom Single Flow, This transaction is to be used if the user initially experiences an error message from the printing rest call or if hazmat documentation is needed to be printed. The RF Print OB docs transaction will attempt to make a successful REST call with the Ecommerce system. The user is able to choose which documents they want to print. The RF Print OB Docs transaction has been enhanced as part of this modification to have custom ECOM print options available to the user. The below custom options are the only options which are supported by the ECOM flow. All Ecom Docs Ecom Ship label Ecom Pack list Ecom Invoice (only works if all LPNs have been weighed) Ecom Hazmat Lastly, the system code C-89P is leveraged to specify which documents will be printed on which print requestors. C-89P-001 is setup for the Document Print Requestor which is used for all PDF documents including the content label and the invoice but does not include the hazmat document. C-89P-002 is setup for the Hazmat Print Requestor C-89P-003 is setup for the Shipping Label Print Requestor Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX89 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX89 is turned off, and no custom EX89 processing will occur. Lastly, upon LPN scan WM checks to see if the LPN belongs to an Order with an Order Type of ECOM. If the LPN is associated with an ECOM order, then the below changes are carried out. If not, then no custom EX89 processing occurs. Validation The b ase WM LPN validations of checking if an LPN is greater than or equal to shipped is suppressed The base WM LPN validations of checking if a LPN’s associated order is greater than or equal to shipped or invoiced is suppressed. Process Whenever the above enablement has passed and the LPN the user has scanned is a n ECOM LPN then the user will be prompted to choose the custom print options. Only the custom print options which are enabled in the transaction parameters are displayed to the user. Any base print options which are enabled will not be displayed to the user (The base options should not be enabled on this transaction). Based on the user ’ s choice of which document they would like to print WM makes a REST call to the McLane Ecommerce system to be sent the appropriate documents. The document type indicator in the JSON file that WM sends is populated with the indicator matching the users document selection. Once WM has received the JSON file it checks the system code C-89P for the corresponding print requestor and sends the files to the printer. Synchronous REST call When carrying out the transaction, the user has the ability to choose which documents they want to print/re-print. Depending on the selection made the Document Type indicator field in the JSON file is appropriately updated to match the selected value /values. Once the document type selections are made WM gathers and sends out the below information in a JSON file through a REST call to the Get Documents API. Ecom order number (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.EXT_PURCHASE_ORDER) associated with the LPN LPN ID # of gel packs by size (if applicable, WM sends 0 for each size if no gel packs needed) LPN container size LPN container weight Items and quantity Last LPN of the shipment indicator WM checks to see if the LPN that has been packed is the last LPN that is less than Weighed status. If the LPN is the last LPN, then WM populates this Last LPN indicator as Y If it is not the last LPN, then WM populates the Last LPN indicator as N Document type indicator The values that can be sent with this field are defined in system code C- 89R Technical Note: In order to make the REST call the following authorization and authentication is required. Key Cloak O-AUTH Token based authentication With a successful connection and authorization made, WM receives back the response of the REST call in a JSON file format. The response contains a JSON file which includes all the Outbound Documentation needed to be printed (given that the document type indicator is set to 001. WM then takes the JSON file and extracts the documents and prints them. The user is then shown CUST8905 Info message that job was submitted for printing . If the response does contain an error, then WM displays CUST8904 Message to the user. The CUST8904 Message will display the Error ID and Message found in the JSON file as well as letting the users know to “ for LPN {X}. Please use RF Print OB Docs to retry print and/or Contact Help Desk ” WM records the error message in the MSG_LOG table If the connection to the API was unsuccessful then the following error messages are shown to the user. If due to HTTP Error 400 display CUST8901 If due to HTTP Error 500 display CUST8902 If due to HTTP Error 404 display CUST8903 WM also records these error message in the MSG_LOG table Updates WM first updates the custom table API_TRANSACTION_LOG with the appropriate information from the REST call. A separate Key value pair in the JSON file will be sent with the final Ship Via the ecommerce system has determined to use. WM will take this value and update the LPN’s Ship Via accordingly before printing the Outbound documents. Navigation Once the user has selected the appropriate option and hits enter the REST call is made. If the REST call is unsuccessful an Error message is shown, when the user exits out of the message, they are brought back to the oLPN prompt screen. If the REST call is successful, and errors are received in the response, then the user is shown the error message. When the user exits out of the message, they are brought back to the oLPN prompt screen. If the REST call is successful and no errors are received in the response If the response JSON file does not include the Hazmat key value pair, then user is shown the CUST8905 Info message indicating that the job was submitted for printing. If the response JSON file DOES include the Hazmat key value pair, then the user is shown the CUST8907 Info message indicating that there is Hazmat documentation to be printed. Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking is needed as part of this extension. PIX No PIX needed for this transaction. LM Hooks A custom labor event n eed s to be created for the RF Print OB transaction as part of this extension. This means that WM needs to create the LM Header information and the LM detail information. There will be one LM Header and one LM detail record created per oLPN print transaction , the labor messages will also process to E_EVNT_SMRY_HDR once they are processed to completion. Process Change/Addition For Ecom Singles flow after the user has packed the contents using the RF Packed Cubed Directed transaction , the user is to go to the Weigh and Manifest UI . I n the Ecom Multi flow, once the user has used the RF manage putwall transaction to empty the putwall cubby and print the oLPN label , the user is to go to the Weigh and Manifest UI. If the LPN needs g el packs , then a REST call will be made upon LPN scan at the Weigh and Manifest oLPN scan screen . If the user receives any error s , t he y will need to take the appropriate actions to remedy the error . If no error message s occur or the LPN does not need gel packs , then the user is brought to the Weigh and Manifest Weigh screen. Here the user is able to see the number of gel packs for each gel pack size type needed in the container ( if any ) . The user is to physicall y add those gel packs to the container. If the user decides that more/less gel packs are needed , then the user can update the gel pack quantities for the appropriate sizes. The user also has the ability to change the container’s weight if necessary. Once the user has confirmed the container is ready to be shipped the user will use the Weigh button to carry out further weight validations as well as print the Ecom Outbound documentation. If any hazmat documentation needs to be printed , then the user will see a message indicating that they are needing to use the RF OB docs transaction to print the additional hazmat documentation. If an error is displayed to the user after the Weigh button is selected, the user can take the appropriate actions and retry the weigh button. If no errors occur the users will be brought to the LPN Summary screen and will be able to scan the next LPN. I f the user receives the CUST890 8 Error message stating that the LPN needs to be split, then the SOP is that the user backs out of the Weigh and Manifest UI and uses the RF Split oLPN transaction to transfer the contents to two new smaller containers or split some of the contents to a smaller container. This error message can come either after the LPN scan or after the Weigh button is used. Reporting , Label, or Document The Outbound Document printing is enhanced as part of the modification and is part of the previously mentioned logic. EX89 – Ecom Document printing and Gel Pack Logic During certain times of year, for ecommerce orders, McLane has a need to include gel packs in the containers that are being shipped. This is to keep the contents from thawing/melting. McLane currently has a gel pack logic called the BOLDTSMITH logic which is being used to determine how many gel packs are needed in a container based off the containers contents as well as the weather forecast. In order for WM to leverage this logic t his extension allows WM to integrate with McLane’s Ecommerce system via REST calls to their APIs to request the number of gel packs for a container when needed.

[Document: MCLN EX89 Ecom Document printing and Gel Pack Logic]

cks are needed in a container based off the containers contents as well as the weather forecast. In order for WM to leverage this logic t his extension allows WM to integrate with McLane’s Ecommerce system via REST calls to their APIs to request the number of gel packs for a container when needed.

For McLane’s ecommerce orders , base ecom EPI as well as base parcel carrier logic is not used. Instead, WM is enhanced to integrate with McLane’s Ecommerce system via REST calls to deliver the appropriate information to McLane’s ecommerce system which will then connect to the appropriate parcel carriers and send WM back the shipping information via the appropriate outbound documentation . WM is also enhanced to take this information and submit it for printing. Specifically, WM’s Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced to make the REST calls to McLane’s Ecommerce system . The Weigh and Manifest UI is also enhanced to display the recommended number of gel packs as well as check against custom weight thresholds. The UI is further enhanced to allow users to enter in the number of gel packs , change the containe r type , and also update the weight if deemed necessary . Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Weigh and Manifest UI MHE N RF Y RF Print OB Docs Reporting N Other Y LPN table , 5 custom fields are created For both the Ecom Multi Flow and the Ecom Singles Flow the Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced to make the REST call to the Ecom/Gel Pack API at the time of oLPN scan to receive the calculated number of gel packs that the BOLTSMITH logic has determined. This call is only made if the LPN needs gel packs. The custom weight thresholds for the carriers are also validated in this logic. I f any Errors are sent back to WM , then the errors will be shown to the user and the user will need to try to scan the LPN again after having taken the appropriate actions . The Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced to include new fields for the different gel pack sizes in order to display to the user what gel packs are needed for the container. The user has the ability to edit these fields and the container weight will update appropriately upon edit. The Weigh and Manifest Weigh button is leveraged to make the REST call to send McLane’s ecommerce system the appropriate information to receive back the outbound documentation. Clicking the Weigh button also triggers some custom weight validations. If the base and custom validations pass , then WM makes the REST call to receive back the outbound documentation.

D epending on the response , WM either shows the appropriate error for the failed response or indicates to the user that the job has been submitted for printing . If the response is received back with any errors or there are any errors in the communication, then an error message is displayed to the user and the user will need to use the RF Print OB Docs transaction later on once the error has been resolved. The RF Print OB Docs transaction is also enhanced to make the REST call to McLane in order to attain the appropriate information for printing of the OB documents. The users will be able to choose which documents they want to print with the RF Print OB Docs transaction . Assumptions All Ecom orders have order type as “ ECOM ” For Ecom orders McLane Hub connect s to Ecom API which runs the Boldtsmith logic and send s the order line level gel pack volume needed for that order line Only Ecom orders will be bridged into WM in “Draft” status . Ecom orders are bridged in with a valid Ship Via EX88 is enabled and is used to move the orders into “Released” status. This modification only applies to LPNs which are part of the Ecom flow . Communication between WM and McLane’s Ecommerce system is done through REST APIs where the communication will take place in JSON file format. McLane’s Ecommerce system has appropriately set up the correct REST APIs for WM to connect to. The PDF documents sent in the JSON file are in Base 64 encoding . The ZPL file sent in the JSON file is sent in a ZPL string . For the Outbound Documentation API REST call t he response JSON contains a separate Key value pair for the concatenated PDF, the shipping label, the Hazmat PDF (if needed ), the invoice, and the ship via. WM printer /print requestor configuration is setup properly to print the Ecom Outbound Documents System code C-89 G is correctly configured, with the code id descriptions matching the gel pack sizes which the ecommerce system will send back in their JSON file response. And with the appropriate code ids enabled. Gel pack weight is specified in system code C-8 9 G param 2 - 5 Currently there is a limit of 5 configurable gel pack sizes for a given division Weight limits for containers are configured in system code C-E89 f or each defined ship via , where the code id represents a ship via name . Shipping labels will always be printed on adhesive label s via a label printer. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX88 population of ORDER_LINE_ITEM.

INTERNAL_ORDER_SEQ_ NBR Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram Not required for this extension UI Weigh and Manifest UI The Weigh and Manifest UI is enhanced to call the Ecom/Gel Pack API whenever a n LPN which needs gel packs is scanned. The number of gel packs will be displayed to the user in new custom fields on the right-hand side of the UI Weigh page . These fields will be editable, and if a user would like to change the number of gel packs they can do so via these fields. The fields represent the different gel pack sizes which are set up in system code C-89G. WM is enhanced to show users a message whenever they make a change to the gel pack fields . When they make a change, WM update s the number of gel packs in the container as well as the containers weight in accordance with the increase or decrease in the number of gel packs. Additionally , the Weigh and Manifest UI W eigh button is enhanced to first carry out base validations and updates and then carry out custom weigh validations. Once these updates occur and validations pass , WM make s a second REST call to a different Ecom/Gel Pack API in order to send the McLane’s Ecommerce system the appropriate information needed in order for the Ecommerce system to communicate with the appropriate logistics party and generate the o utbound documentation . The outbound documentation is sent to WM in JSON file and depending on the information WM prints the appropriate documents. If the LPN is the last LPN for a shipment, then WM indicates this to McLane’s Ecommerce system and WM will receive back the Invoice for the entire order along with the other document s for th at LPN. If there are any errors during the REST call or included in the REST call’s response, WM displays this to the user. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled.

Process Change/Addition For Ecom Singles flow after the user has packed the contents using the RF Packed Cubed Directed transaction , the user is to go to the Weigh and Manifest UI . I n the Ecom Multi flow, once the user has used the RF manage putwall transaction to empty the putwall cubby and print the oLPN label , the user is to go to the Weigh and Manifest UI. If the LPN needs g el packs , then a REST call will be made upon LPN scan at the Weigh and Manifest oLPN scan screen . If the user receives any error s , t he y will need to take the appropriate actions to remedy the error . If no error message s occur or the LPN does not need gel packs , then the user is brought to the Weigh and Manifest Weigh screen. Here the user is able to see the number of gel packs for each gel pack size type needed in the container ( if any ) . The user is to physicall y add those gel packs to the container. If the user decides that more/less gel packs are needed , then the user can update the gel pack quantities for the appropriate sizes. The user also has the ability to change the container’s weight if necessary.

Once the user has confirmed the container is ready to be shipped the user will use the Weigh button to carry out further weight validations as well as print the Ecom Outbound documentation. If any hazmat documentation needs to be printed , then the user will see a message indicating that they are needing to use the RF OB docs transaction to print the additional hazmat documentation. If an error is displayed to the user after the Weigh button is selected, the user can take the appropriate actions and retry the weigh button. If no errors occur the users will be brought to the LPN Summary screen and will be able to scan the next LPN. I f the user receives the CUST890 8 Error message stating that the LPN needs to be split, then the SOP is that the user backs out of the Weigh and Manifest UI and uses the RF Split oLPN transaction to transfer the contents to two new smaller containers or split some of the contents to a smaller container. This error message can come either after the LPN scan or after the Weigh button is used.
